steps.title: Substitute g(x) = 49 into the function definition
steps.content:
Set up the equation by substituting into the function definition:
$$x^2 + 13 = 49$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract 13 from both sides:
$$x^2 = 36$$
Taking the square root:
$$x = \pm 6$$
The solutions are $x = 6$ and $x = -6$. Of these, only $x = 6$ appears in the choices. A is correct.
